higher_order_handoff.first_compound_coordinate: The subsequent order-three gate proves that the first contiguous compound sign is exactly C_n=q_(n+1)*q_(n+3)-q_(n+2)^2+1>0.
higher_order_handoff.first_compound_guard: A strict rational sequence satisfies the full ratio, adaptive-defect, and cubic Jensen cones while having C_n<0.
higher_order_handoff.forbidden_promotion: Karlin's fixed-scale sign regularity and the scalar CV_n bound do not imply mixture sign regularity in orders k>=3.
higher_order_handoff.target: Control the sign-changing row-wise compound integrand after Xi mixing for every k and column set, using a hierarchy of tilted concentration, a coupling that keeps row scales near the diagonal, or a new positive compound-kernel factorization.

karlin_sign_regularity.citation: S. Karlin, Total positivity, absorption probabilities and applications, Trans. Amer. Math. Soc. 111 (1964), Lemma 9.2
karlin_sign_regularity.scope: This proves the complete fixed-scale antecedent, including arbitrary increasing row and column sets.
karlin_sign_regularity.specialization: For every t>0 and n>=0, K_(n,t)(i,j)=gamma_(n+i+j)*t^(n+i+j) is strictly sign-regular of all orders with signature epsilon_k=(-1)^(k*(k-1)/2).
karlin_sign_regularity.theorem: For alpha>-nu, every (nu+1)-minor of [1/Gamma(alpha+i+j)]_(i,j>=0) has strict sign (-1)^(nu*(nu+1)/2).

positive_mixture_countermodel.conclusion: Positive mixing of strictly sign-regular fixed-scale reciprocal-gamma kernels is not closed even at order two, with all scales strictly positive.
positive_mixture_countermodel.expected: The signed-Hankel order-two sign would require A_0*A_2-A_1^2<0.

rowwise_mixture_integral.boundary: The scales are independent, so Karlin's common-scale sign theorem does not sign this integrand away from the diagonal t_0=...=t_(k-1).
rowwise_mixture_integral.derivation: Expand the determinant by multilinearity in its rows and assign one independent scale variable to each row.

tilted_concentration.scope: This closes all shifted order-two signed-Hankel minors only.
tilted_concentration.xi_input: The lambda=-100 full ratio-cone entry plus infinite heat-flow invariance proves this tilted relative-variance bound for every n at lambda=0.

proof_boundary: This artifact applies Karlin's published all-order sign-regularity theorem to the fixed-scale reciprocal-gamma kernel, proves the exact row-wise determinant integral for positive Xi scale mixtures, and exhibits exact sign-changing integrands and a strictly-positive-scale mixture countermodel. It translates the established Xi order-two cone into a tilted relative-variance bound. It does not prove higher-order mixture sign regularity, the signed-Hankel/Jensen bridge, PF-infinity, RH, or Lambda<=0.

rows.claim: The normalized Xi coefficients are positive scale mixtures of the reciprocal-gamma sequence.
Karlin's reciprocal-gamma kernel is strictly sign-regular in every order.

diagnostics.scope: This proves the complete fixed-scale antecedent, including arbitrary increasing row and column sets.
diagnostics.specialization: For every t>0 and n>=0, K_(n,t)(i,j)=gamma_(n+i+j)*t^(n+i+j) is strictly sign-regular of all orders with signature epsilon_k=(-1)^(k*(k-1)/2).
diagnostics.theorem: For alpha>-nu, every (nu+1)-minor of [1/Gamma(alpha+i+j)]_(i,j>=0) has strict sign (-1)^(nu*(nu+1)/2).

rows.formula: For alpha>-nu, every (nu+1)-minor of [1/Gamma(alpha+i+j)]_(i,j>=0) has strict sign (-1)^(nu*(nu+1)/2).
rows.proof_boundary: Published fixed-kernel theorem; mixture closure is not part of the result.

rows.claim: Every positive fixed-scale Xi coefficient kernel has exactly the observed all-order signature.
rows.formula: For every t>0 and n>=0, K_(n,t)(i,j)=gamma_(n+i+j)*t^(n+i+j) is strictly sign-regular of all orders with signature epsilon_k=(-1)^(k*(k-1)/2).

rows.claim: The contiguous reciprocal-gamma Hankel determinants have an explicit strict product formula.
rows.proof_boundary: Exact fixed-scale benchmark, not a mixed-measure determinant.

rows.claim: Every shifted reshaped-Hankel minor has an exact independent-row scale integral.

diagnostics.boundary: The scales are independent, so Karlin's common-scale sign theorem does not sign this integrand away from the diagonal t_0=...=t_(k-1).
diagnostics.derivation: Expand the determinant by multilinearity in its rows and assign one independent scale variable to each row.

rows.proof_boundary: The exact integrand is signed; no positive determinant formula is claimed.

rows.claim: The natural symmetrized order-two determinant integrand changes sign away from the common-scale diagonal.
rows.proof_boundary: Rejects pointwise positivity of the natural row-wise integral only.

rows.claim: A positive three-atom measure on strictly positive scales reverses the required order-two sign.

diagnostics.conclusion: Positive mixing of strictly sign-regular fixed-scale reciprocal-gamma kernels is not closed even at order two, with all scales strictly positive.
diagnostics.expected: The signed-Hankel order-two sign would require A_0*A_2-A_1^2<0.

rows.proof_boundary: Abstract positive-mixture countermodel, not the Xi measure.

rows.claim: The complete shifted degree-two cone is exactly a shrinking relative-variance bound under moment tilting.

diagnostics.scope: This closes all shifted order-two signed-Hankel minors only.

rows.claim: The established ratio-cone entry and heat invariance supply the tilted relative-variance theorem for Xi at lambda zero.
rows.formula: The lambda=-100 full ratio-cone entry plus infinite heat-flow invariance proves this tilted relative-variance bound for every n at lambda=0.
rows.proof_boundary: All shifts at order two; no higher compound sign is inferred.

rows.claim: The remaining signed-Hankel antecedent is a higher-order concentration or positive compound-kernel theorem for the Xi mixing measure.

status: exact reciprocal-gamma sign regularity with positive-mixture obstruction
